mission.title: Install and Configure Ingress NGINX Controller
mission.description: Ingress NGINX Controller is a widely-used Ingress controller for Kubernetes that utilizes NGINX as a reverse proxy and load balancer. It is suitable for routing external traffic to services within a Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Install Ingress NGINX Controller
steps.description:
Use Helm to install the Ingress NGINX Controller. Ensure you have Helm v3.x installed. Run the following commands:

```bash
# Add the ingress-nginx repository
helm repo add ingress-nginx https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/main/deploy/helm-chart
helm repo update

# Install the Ingress NGINX Controller
helm install ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --version v1.12.0 --namespace ingress-nginx --create-namespace
```

Verify the installation:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx
kubectl get svc -n ingress-nginx
```

Ensure all pods are running and services are available.

steps.title: Configure Ingress Resource
steps.description:
Create an Ingress resource to route traffic to your services. Save the following YAML to `ingress.yaml`:

```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: default
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
```

Apply the Ingress resource:

```bash
kubectl apply -f ingress.yaml
```

Check the Ingress status:

```bash
kubectl get ingress
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ingress NGINX Controller
uninstall.description:
To uninstall the Ingress NGINX Controller, run the following command:

```bash
helm uninstall ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx
```

Cleanup the namespace if no longer needed:

```bash
kubectl delete namespace ingress-nginx
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ingress NGINX Controller
upgrade.description:
To upgrade the Ingress NGINX Controller, first backup your configuration:

```bash
kubectl get ingress -o yaml > ingress-backup.yaml
```

Then, upgrade using Helm:

```bash
helm upgrade ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --version v1.12.0 --namespace ingress-nginx
```

Verify the upgrade:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx
```

If issues arise, rollback to the previous version:

```bash
helm rollback ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx
```

troubleshooting.title: Ingress Not Routing Traffic
troubleshooting.description:
If your Ingress is not routing traffic, check the following:
- Ensure the Ingress resource is correctly defined with the right service name and port.
- Verify the NGINX controller pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx
```
- Check the logs of the NGINX controller:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=ingress-nginx -n ingress-nginx
```

troubleshooting.title: TLS Not Working
troubleshooting.description:
If TLS is not functioning, ensure that the TLS secret exists in the same namespace as the Ingress resource. Check with:
```bash
kubectl get secret -n default
```
- Ensure the Ingress resource specifies the correct secret name under `tls`.

troubleshooting.title: 404 Not Found Error
troubleshooting.description:
A 404 error may indicate that the Ingress resource is not matching the request. Verify the host and path in your Ingress resource match the incoming request. Check the Ingress definition with:
```bash
kubectl describe ingress example-ingress
```

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for custom NGINX templates and enhanced metrics collection.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for external authentication and improved performance.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of the Ingress NGINX Controller includes running pods in the ingress-nginx namespace and a functioning Ingress resource that routes traffic to your services. Ensure that your Ingress rules are correctly defined and that the controller is properly configured.
codeSnippets:
# Add the ingress-nginx repository
helm repo add ingress-nginx https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/main/deploy/helm-chart
helm repo update

# Install the Ingress NGINX Controller
helm install ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --version v1.12.0 --namespace ingress-nginx --create-namespace

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx
kubectl get svc -n ingress-nginx

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: default
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and Helm installed.
